revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tagitsuhime]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。[[古事記]]では'''多岐都比売命'''、[[日本書紀]]では'''湍津姫'''と表記される。
==神話における記述== [[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの持つ剣を譲り受けて宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの物実から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、どの宮に祀られるかは、記紀で異同がある。 *古事記・日本書紀第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *日本書紀本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。 [[宗像大社]]では、「湍津姫神」として中津宮に祀られている。
[[先代旧事本紀]]には、後に[[大国主|大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[事代主|八重事代主神]]と高照光姫命を生んだと記されている。
==解説== 神名の「タギツ」は「滾（たぎ）つ」（水が激しく流れる）の意で、[[天の安河]]の早瀬のことと解釈される。
タギツヒメを単独で祀る神社は少なく、宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた五男三女神とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
[[Category:日本の神|たきつひめ]] [[Category:海神|たきつひめ]]

'''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。[[古事記]]では'''多岐都比売命'''、[[日本書紀]]では'''湍津姫'''と表記される。
==神話における記述== [[Image:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（古事記に基づく） [[media:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]

[[先代旧事本紀]]には、後に[[大国主|大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[事代主|八重事代主神]]と高照光姫命を生んだと記されている。

タギツヒメを単独で祀る神社は少なく、宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた五男三女神とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。

==神話における記述== [[Image:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの持つ剣を譲り受けて宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの物実から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、どの宮に祀られるかは、『記紀』で異同がある。 *『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では、「湍津姫神」として大島の中津宮に祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』には、後に[[大国主|大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[事代主|八重事代主神]]と高照光姫命を生んだと記されている。
== 解説 == 神名の「タギツ」は「滾（たぎ）つ」（水が激しく流れる）の意で、[[天の安河]]の早瀬のことと解釈される。
タギツヒメを単独で祀る神社は少なく、宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

== 系譜 == [[天照大神]]と[[須佐之男命]]の誓約で生まれた宗像三女神の一柱。 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』には、後に[[大国主|大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[事代主|八重事代主神]]と高照光姫命を生んだと記されている。 なお『[[海部氏勘注系図]]』に神屋多底姫命（かむやたてひめ）の別名として多岐津姫命の名が取り上げられていることから、『古事記』で大国主との間に事代主を生んだ[[神屋楯比売]]（かむやたてひめ）との関連性も疑われる。 {{独自研究|section=1|date=2018年3月}}<!--「主な」たる根拠が不明のまま、執筆者の主観のみをもって列挙が続けられているため。（何らかの根拠に則って記述する必要がある。ノート参照！）-->
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像三女神]] *[[比売神]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

{{神道}} '''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。『[[古事記]]』では'''多岐都比売命'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''湍津姫'''と表記される。
==神話における記述== [[Image:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの持つ剣を譲り受けて宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの物実から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、どの宮に祀られるかは、『記紀』で異同がある。 *『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では、「湍津姫神」として大島の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == 神名の「タギツ」は「滾（たぎ）つ」（水が激しく流れる）の意で、[[天の安河]]の早瀬のことと解釈される。
タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。 また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
== 系譜 == [[天照大神]]と[[須佐之男命]]の誓約で生まれた宗像三女神の一柱。 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』には、後に[[大国主|大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[事代主|八重事代主神]]と高照光姫命を生んだと記されている。 なお『[[海部氏勘注系図]]』に神屋多底姫命（かむやたてひめ）の別名として多岐津姫命の名が取り上げられていることから、『古事記』で大国主との間に事代主を生んだ[[神屋楯比売]]（かむやたてひめ）との関連性も疑われる。 {{独自研究|section=1|date=2018年3月}}<!--「主な」たる根拠が不明のまま、執筆者の主観のみをもって列挙が続けられているため。（何らかの根拠に則って記述する必要がある。ノート参照！）-->
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像三女神]] *[[比売神]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

[[宗像大社]]では日本書紀本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。

revision.comment: 海部氏勘注系図は、あくまで国宝海部氏本系図の「附けたり」（江戸時代に創作された説明書）でありその信憑性には疑問があり、これをタギツヒメの系譜などとするのは問題がある。異論があればノートで議論を。

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須枳高日子]]の子である多伎都比古命の妻とされる。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多岐都比売命 | 世代名 = [[宗像三女神]] | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など --> | 全名 = 多岐都比売命 | 別名 = 多伎都比売命、湍津姫、高津姫神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[須佐之男命]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]]（多伎都比古命） | 子 = [[事代主神]]、[[高照光姫大神命]] | 宮 = 辺津宮または中津宮 | 神社 = [[宗像大社]]辺津宮、等 }} '''多岐都比売命'''（タギツヒメノミコト、タキツヒメとも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。
==神話における記述== [[Image:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[media:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの[[十束剣]]を口に含み、三つにかみ砕き吐き出した霧から宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの[[物実]]から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。 この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、三つの内でどの宮に祀られるかは、『[[記紀]]』で異同がある。
*『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では日本書紀本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。 また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須枳高日子]]の子である多伎都比古命の妻とされる。

[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの[[十束剣]]を口に含み、三つにかみ砕き吐き出した霧から宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの[[物実]]から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。 この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、三つの内でどの宮に祀られるかは、『[[記紀]]』で異同がある。
*『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では日本書紀本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。 また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須枳高日子]]の子である多伎都比古命の妻とされる。
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像三女神]] *[[比売神]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多岐都比売命 | 世代名 = [[宗像三女神]] | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など --> | 全名 = 多岐都比売命 | 別名 = 多岐津比売命、湍津姫命、高津姫神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[須佐之男命]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]]（多岐都比古命） | 子 = [[事代主神]]、[[高照光姫命]] | 宮 = 辺津宮または中津宮 | 神社 = [[宗像大社]]辺津宮、等 }} '''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。『[[古事記]]』では'''多岐都比売命'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''湍津姫命'''と表記される<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://discoverjapan-web.com/article/42022|title=スサノオの剣から誕生した水の神様「多紀理毘売命」「市寸島比売命」「多岐都比売命」|date=2020-12-07 |publisher=[[ディスカバー・ジャパン]] |accessdate=2022-12-26}}</ref>。
== 神話における記述 == [[Image:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[メディア:Challenge of Amaterashu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの[[十束剣]]を口に含み、三つにかみ砕き吐き出した霧から宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの[[物実]]から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。
この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、三つの内でどの宮に祀られるかは、『[[記紀]]』で異同がある。
*『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では『日本書紀』本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。
また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅鉏高日子根]]の子である多岐都比古命の妻とされる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[宗像三女神]] * [[比売神]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{draft}}{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Takiribime|ja|タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ}}|children=[[Kotoshironushi]]}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ}} [[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] }} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]]"
added [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]

revision.text:
{{draft}}{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Takiribime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft}} is one of three female kami (deities) in Japanese mythology. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities Amaterasu and Susanoo. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's honesty. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the Munakata and Minuma clans enshrined them in Tsukushi, now part of Kyushu, Japan. They became known as the "three goddesses of Munakata."<ref name=":0" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the Genkai Sea.<ref name=":0" />
[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References ==

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft}} is one of three female kami (deities) in Japanese mythology. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's honesty. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the Munakata and Minuma clans enshrined them in Tsukushi, now part of Kyushu, Japan. They became known as the "three goddesses of Munakata."<ref name=":0" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the Genkai Sea.<ref name=":0" />
[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]]

{{Expand French|date=2024年4月}}{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多岐都比売命 | 世代名 = [[宗像三女神]] | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など --> | 全名 = 多岐都比売命 | 別名 = 多岐津比売命、湍津姫命、高津姫神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[須佐之男命]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]]（多岐都比古命） | 子 = [[事代主神]]、[[高照光姫命]] | 宮 = 辺津宮または中津宮 | 神社 = [[宗像大社]]辺津宮、等 }} '''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。『[[古事記]]』では'''多岐都比売命'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''湍津姫命'''と表記される<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://discoverjapan-web.com/article/42022|title=スサノオの剣から誕生した水の神様「多紀理毘売命」「市寸島比売命」「多岐都比売命」|date=2020-12-07 |publisher=[[ディスカバー・ジャパン]] |accessdate=2022-12-26}}</ref>。
== 神話における記述 == [[Image:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[メディア:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの[[十束剣]]を口に含み、三つにかみ砕き吐き出した霧から宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの[[物実]]から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。
この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、三つの内でどの宮に祀られるかは、『[[記紀]]』で異同がある。
*『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では『日本書紀』本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。
また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅鉏高日子根]]の子である多岐都比古命の妻とされる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[宗像三女神]] * [[比売神]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with Lakshmi in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] }} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <references />{{Munakata Faith}} [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with Lakshmi in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] }} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <references />{{Munakata Faith}} [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

revision.text:
{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]}} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]]
[[Category:bump]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <references />{{Munakata Faith}} [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]

She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]}} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]]

revision.text:
{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]}} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]]
[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <references />{{Munakata Faith}} [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. revision.text:
{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]}} [[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]]
[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tagitsuhime]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:タギツヒメ]] [[simple: user:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime]]
== References == [[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <references />{{Munakata Faith}} [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]]

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tagitsuhime]][[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tagitsuhime]][[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
