[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tagitsuhime]][[en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}[[fr:Tagitsuhime]] {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|user:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi|en}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]] }} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en}}, now in the area around [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|Munakata Sanjojin}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|en:draft||fr}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多岐都比売命 | 世代名 = [[宗像三女神]] | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など --> | 全名 = 多岐都比売命 | 別名 = 多岐津比売命、湍津姫命、高津姫神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[須佐之男命]] | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]]（多岐都比古命） | 子 = [[事代主神]]、[[高照光姫命]] | 宮 = 辺津宮または中津宮 | 神社 = [[宗像大社]]辺津宮、等 }} '''タギツヒメ'''（'''タキツヒメ'''とも）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|女神]]で、[[宗像三女神]]の一柱である。『[[古事記]]』では'''多岐都比売命'''、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''湍津姫命'''と表記される<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://discoverjapan-web.com/article/42022|title=スサノオの剣から誕生した水の神様「多紀理毘売命」「市寸島比売命」「多岐都比売命」|date=2020-12-07 |publisher=[[ディスカバー・ジャパン]] |accessdate=2022-12-26}}</ref>。
== 神話における記述 == [[Image:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo-ja.svg|thumb|right|240px|アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約（『古事記』に基づく） [[メディア:Children born from the Challenge of Amaterasu and Susanoo-ja.svg|SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]]]
[[アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]]の段で、アマテラスがスサノオの[[十束剣]]を口に含み、三つにかみ砕き吐き出した霧から宗像三女神を生み、スサノオの[[物実]]から生まれたのでスサノオの子であると宣言された。
この三女神は宗像の民が信仰している神であると記されている。化生した順番や、三つの内でどの宮に祀られるかは、『[[記紀]]』で異同がある。
*『古事記』・『日本書紀』第二の一書では、3番目に化生し、辺津宮に祀られる。 *『日本書紀』本文・第一の一書・第三の一書では、2番目に化生し、中津宮に祀られる。
[[宗像大社]]では『日本書紀』本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。
== 解説 == タギツヒメを主祭神として祀る神社は中津宮のほか、[[青森県]]の[[岩木山神社]]など。
また宗像三女神の一柱として各地の[[宗像大社|宗像神社]]・[[厳島神社]]などで、また、アマテラスとスサノオの誓約で生まれた[[五男三女神]]とともに各地の[[八王子神社]]などで祀られている。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では後に[[大己貴神]]に嫁ぎ、[[八重事代主神]]と[[高照光姫命]]を生んだと記されている。また出雲では[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅鉏高日子根]]の子である多岐都比古命の妻とされる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[宗像三女神]] * [[比売神]]
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきつひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:海神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:八幡信仰]]

[[宗像大社]]では『日本書紀』本文の通り「湍津姫神」として[[宗像市]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]の中津宮に祀られている。[[御神体]]の依代は[[有職故実]]に「紫玉」と記述されている。

While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}

{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG
| Hinduism_equivalent = [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]])
| equivalent1_type = Buddhist
| equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saihajin|qid=Q60156}}}}

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]]
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

== Syncretism ==
Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.

However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]

{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG
| Hinduism_equivalent = [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]])
| equivalent1_type = Buddhist

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]]
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

== Syncretism ==
Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.

However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]]
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

== Syncretism ==
Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.

However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]]
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

== Syncretism ==
Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.

However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]

She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}
[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]]
Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

== Syncretism ==
Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.

However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]
== See Also ==

* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saihajin|WD=Q60156}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tagitsuhime]][[ja:タギツヒメ]][[simple:Tagitsu-hime]][[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tagitsuhime]][[ja:タギツヒメ]][[simple:Tagitsu-hime]][[zh:多岐都比賣命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tagitsuhime]][[ja:タギツヒメ]][[simple:Tagitsu-hime]][[zh:多岐都比賣命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10932592]] Wikidata name: Tagitsuhime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tagitsuhime]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saihajin|WD=Q60156}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakṣmī]] (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saihajin|WD=Q60156}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's [[:en:Honesty|honesty]]. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with [[:en:Lakshmi|Lakshmi]] or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. {{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakṣmī}} (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saihajin|WD=Q60156}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's {{ill|Honesty|en|Honesty|WD=Q337349|lt=honesty}}. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakṣmī}} (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saiha Shin|WD=Q60156}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's {{ill|Honesty|en|Honesty|WD=Q337349|lt=honesty}}. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}
