{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakṣmī|1=Lakshmi}} (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saiha Shin|WD=Q60156|1=Saiha Shin}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's {{ill|Honesty|en|Honesty|WD=Q337349|lt=honesty|1=Honesty}}. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==

There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|father=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]|spouse=[[Ōkuninushi]]|cult_centre=[[Munakata Taisha]]|siblings={{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}|children={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}|name=Tagitsuhime|caption=Nakatsu-gū (中津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Nakatsu Shrine).JPG | Hinduism_equivalent = {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakṣmī|1=Lakshmi}} (via [[Kisshōten]]) | equivalent1_type = Buddhist | equivalent1 = [[Kisshōten]]|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}|consort={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}|offspring={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}|member_of={{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|WD=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Saiha Shin|WD=Q60156|1=Saiha Shin}}}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|en:draft|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} is one of three female kami (deities) in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She was born from a ritual called "ukei," a trial by oath, involving the deities [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. As part of this ritual, Amaterasu broke Susanoo's sword into pieces, washed the fragments in the True Well of Heaven, chewed them, and then expelled them. Tagitsuhime emerged from this process.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9150|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
The birth of three female deities from this trial was seen as proof of Susanoo's {{ill|Honesty|en|Honesty|WD=Q337349|lt=honesty|1=Honesty}}. Following this event, Amaterasu ordered the three deities to descend to Earth. There, the {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} and Minuma clan built shrines for them in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi Province|qq=}}, now in the area around {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. They became known as the {{Nihongo|three goddesses of Munakata|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Sanjojin|qq=draft}}<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}} [[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}]] Currently, Tagitsuhime is worshipped at Nakatsugū, which is one of the three shrines within the Munakata Taisha shrine complex. These three goddesses are considered protectors of ocean travelers, especially in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|lt=Sea of Genkai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />
== Syncretism == Tagorihime has been identified with {{ill|Lakshmi|en|Lakshmi|WD=Q131443|lt=Lakshmi|1=Lakshmi}} or [[Kisshōten]] in syncretism of the Munakata Goddesses with the Hindu Tridevi. As she is the maintainer and the present, she is the intermediate distance island from the coast.
However this worship is rare and much less common than syncretism of just {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} with Saraswati[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]] == See Also ==
